summary: Comprehensive backend (44 pytest tests, 100% pass) + frontend Playwright e2e of Inventory Management System. All 16 review-request features verified end-to-end: JWT auth (admin/manager/staff), RBAC, Categories/Suppliers/Products CRUD with SKU-uniqueness, bulk-delete, Excel import, Excel+PDF exports, Inventory stock-in/out with insufficient-stock guard, dashboard report (kpi + 4 charts), low-stock + send-alert (Resend), monthly+transactions reports, report Excel exports (low_stock/transactions/audit/products), audit logs with staff-403, supplier detail endpoint. Frontend: login → dashboard with 7 KPI cards + 4 charts, products full CRUD, inventory stock-in updates qty, all pages route correctly, audit trail UI shows all captured actions.

issues: Delete confirmation uses native window.confirm() in /app/frontend/src/pages/Products.jsx (lines 117, 128) instead of shadcn AlertDialog already imported in components/ui/alert-dialog.jsx. Native dialog breaks visual consistency with the emerald theme and is not accessible/themeable. Recommend swapping to AlertDialog.
React console warning: 'Missing Description or aria-describedby={undefined} for {DialogContent}'. Add DialogDescription (or aria-describedby) inside DialogContent for accessibility.

action_items: (Optional) Replace window.confirm with shadcn AlertDialog on Products page for theme consistency
(Optional) Add DialogDescription inside product/category/supplier dialogs to silence a11y warning
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~742 lines - acceptable but approaching the 700-line guideline; consider splitting into routers (auth_router, products_router, reports_router) for maintainability.

Products list `stock_status` filter is applied AFTER pagination (server.py ~262-267) which can return fewer items than `limit` while `total` still reflects unfiltered count - leads to incorrect pagination math on the client. Move stock_status into the Mongo query (or compute via $expr with $lte) or paginate after filtering.
Monthly-growth (server.py ~562-567) uses 30-day arithmetic to compute month-prefixes, which drifts across long months (e.g. Mar/May). Use dateutil.relativedelta or explicit month subtraction.
send-alert endpoint (server.py ~608) treats q<=reorder as low-stock including out-of-stock; the GET /reports/low-stock splits them (0<q<=r vs q==0). Behavior is acceptable but slightly inconsistent.
_check_and_alert_low_stock fires Resend email on EVERY stock-out that crosses threshold without any throttling/dedupe; can spam recipients. Recommend a per-product cooldown or daily digest.

auth.py: hash_password uses bcrypt.gensalt() default (12 rounds) - good. Verified bcrypt hash format starts with $2b$ in DB (via library). No load_dotenv timing issues observed; .env loaded before importing JWT_SECRET.

CORS_ORIGINS='*' combined with allow_credentials=True is invalid per CORS spec; browsers will refuse credentialed requests from real origins. Restrict origins or drop credentials. (Currently using Bearer header, so not breaking auth, but the misconfig should be fixed.)
No brute-force/rate-limit on /auth/login - the auth playbook expects lockout after ~5 fails. Missing.
No login attempt counter / lockout / IP throttling implemented.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on tested flows (login, dashboard charts, products CRUD, inventory stock-in, navigation across all pages, audit trail)

test_credentials: admin@inventory.com/Admin@123, manager@inventory.com/Manager@123, staff@inventory.com/Staff@123 - all verified working
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_-prefixed categories/suppliers/products and clean them up via bulk-delete + delete endpoints in TestCleanup. A few transient audit-log entries remain (expected - audit is append-only).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All backend endpoints in the review request pass. backend_test.py is structured by feature (TestAuth, TestCategories, TestSuppliers, TestProducts, TestInventory, TestReports, TestAudit, TestCleanup) and uses session-scoped fixtures for tokens. Native window.confirm() is used on Products page delete, so Playwright tests must register page.on('dialog', lambda d: d.accept()) BEFORE clicking delete. CORS_ORIGINS='*' + allow_credentials=True and no login rate-limit are open items noted in code review.
rca of the issue: No blocking bugs found. The one initial test failure (UI delete leaving sku visible) was due to my Playwright script not handling the native window.confirm dialog - re-running with page.on('dialog', d.accept()) confirmed delete works correctly. Underlying recommendation is to migrate from window.confirm to shadcn AlertDialog (already present in the codebase) for theme/a11y consistency.
